latest.title: Magnets make invisible fields visible
latest.script_key: magnets can show a hidden force map watch the filings line up because each tiny piece becomes a small magnet in the field together they trace the direction of the force around the magnet the pattern is physics drawing itself which force next

latest.title: Cats groom to reset their scent map
latest.script_key: cats use grooming to reset their scent map watch the tongue and paw movements because grooming spreads familiar scent across the coat that can help a cat settle after a tense moment and keep the fur ready for the next move the cleanup is also a signal which pet behavior should we decode next

latest.title: Mountain trails trick you with hidden time

latest.title: Mountains breathe steam not smoke
latest.script_key: mountains exhale steam not smoke see that white haze over the peak that s superheated water hitting cold air that s why glaciers carve valleys they re not just ice they re giant water cannons did you think those white clouds were just weather

latest.title: Storm clouds show air layers changing over time
latest.script_key: storm clouds show air layers changing over time watch the cloud deck because winds at different heights can push each layer in a different direction a timelapse turns that invisible motion into a map which sky motion should we read next

latest.title: Elephants detect changes with their eyes
latest.script_key: elephants detect changes with their eyes watch the eyes first because that small signal shows where attention shifts before the body commits the clip looks simple but timing turns it into survival information which elephant clue should we decode next

latest.title: fox night: how city foxes beat traffic
latest.script_key: foxes don t see red or green just slow motion blurs that s why they wait for gaps not lights watch this one twice the fox darts when the car lights flicker not when the cars stop that s because headlights are brighter than the road would you cross that road

latest.title: Koalas cheat the climbing rule with built-in grip
latest.script_key: koalas cheat the climbing rule with built in grip see those two thumbs on each paw most animals have five digits koalas have six built for bark that s why they can nap in a tree all day without gripping would you trust your life to that grip

latest.title: Coral reefs are built by tiny living builders
latest.script_key: coral reefs are built by tiny living builders watch the reef texture because coral animals build hard skeletons that stack into habitat fish algae and countless small creatures then use that structure like a city the color sits on top of engineering which habitat next
